events.title: Pakistan rejects Amnesty call for war crimes probe into Kabul airstrike
events.description: Pakistan has rejected Amnesty International’s call for an investigation into a March airstrike on a drug rehabilitation center in the Afghan capital that the rights group said could amount to a war crime, dismissing the allegations as unsupported
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pakistan rejects Amnesty call for war crimes probe into Kabul airstrike

events.title: 25 dead in India tunnel disaster as more bodies recovered
events.description: The bodies of the remaining 13 workers trapped inside a collapsed hydropower tunnel in India have been found - as the death toll rises to 25.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 25 dead in India tunnel disaster as more bodies recovered

events.title: Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre
events.description: A 600-square-meter exhibition features Hamas tunnel replicas, attack vehicles, and documents revealing Hezbollah and Iran's roles in the Oct. 7 assault.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre

events.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
events.description: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 59th Asean foreign minister’s meeting is being held – will also caution world leaders against placing a toll on the strait of Hormuz after both Iran and the US threatened to do amid the protracted conflict. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal

events.title: Spain declares an emergency after wildfires force evacuations of 10,000 people
events.description: Spain’s government has declared a national emergency Friday after multiple wildfires forced the evacuation of some 10,000 people in the central region of Madrid
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Spain declares an emergency after wildfires force evacuations of 10,000 people

events.title: Trump threatens Iran and Houthi rebels with 'major military punishment'
events.description: Iran and the United States threatened to intensify attacks on Thursday after Yemen's Houthi rebels struck Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, driving Brent crude above $100 a barrel and fuelling fears of a widening regional conflict and renewed pressure on the global economy.

events.title: World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia
events.description: Independent UN human rights experts on Tuesday condemned the M23 armed group’s “reign of terror” over territory it controls in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), calling for an end to the Rwanda-backed group’s sexual torture and violence against civilians.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia

events.title: University courses covering Israel-Palestine should be audited, Julian Leeser tells royal commission
events.description: Inquiry’s focus this week is on security for the Jewish community, with the MP telling the commissioner his son is afraid to attend synagogues and events Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The shadow education minister, Julian Leeser, has called for universities to undertake an audit of any classes that teach about the Jewish community, Israel or Palestine. Appearing before the royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion on Friday, Leeser said he had been “very concerned” about antisemitism on university campuses and was not satisfied with how vice-chancellors had addressed the issue after the 7 October 2023 Hamas terrorist attack. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 University courses covering Israel-Palestine should be audited, Julian Leeser tells royal commission

events.title: France orders total evacuation of Cap Ferret peninsula as wildfires sweep western Europe
events.description: Wildfires fuelled by extreme heat and strong winds swept across western Europe on Friday, forcing French authorities to order the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula tourism hotspot. The blazes also triggered a state of emergency in Spain's Madrid region and prompted Paris to seek emergency assistance from the European Union.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France orders total evacuation of Cap Ferret peninsula as wildfires sweep western Europe

events.title: Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors
events.description: Flash floods have killed at least 26 people across eastern Afghanistan, including 23 in the mountainous province of Nuristan, where homes, markets and roads were swept away and search teams continued looking for people reported missing.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors

events.title: Jürgen Klopp named new Germany coach after World Cup disappointment
events.description: Germany has turned to Jürgen Klopp to lead a new era for the four-time world champions after their disappointing World Cup exit, with hopes of building toward future international tournaments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jürgen Klopp named new Germany coach after World Cup disappointment

events.title: Four Palestinians and one Israeli killed in occupied West Bank shooting
events.description: Four Palestinians and an Israeli were killed in a shooting in the Israeli-occupied West Bank Friday, Israeli and Palestinian officials said. Violence between Israeli settlers and troops and Palestinians has risen sharply in the occupied Palestinian territory since the start of the Gaza war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Four Palestinians and one Israeli killed in occupied West Bank shooting

events.title: Ukraine drone attack sparks fire at Russian e-commerce giant's warehouse near Saint Petersburg
events.description: At least three people were wounded after a Ukrainian drone attack sparked a fire at a warehouse belonging to Russian e-commerce giant Wildberries near Saint Petersburg early Friday, regional officials said. Kyiv has struck five Wildberries warehouses since July 18, killing eight night-shift workers in a warehouse outside Moscow on Saturday and wounding dozens more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine drone attack sparks fire at Russian e-commerce giant's warehouse near Saint Petersburg

events.title: ICC drops war crimes case against fugitive Sudan rebel Abdallah Banda
events.description: International Criminal Court judges have reluctantly dropped the case against a Sudanese rebel accused of attacking peacekeepers in Darfur nearly 20 years ago
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ICC drops war crimes case against fugitive Sudan rebel Abdallah Banda

events.title: Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls
events.description: As the Iran-US war continues, the key question remains unanswered about how shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will be managed in future – although the UN has reiterated that charging tolls for safe passage runs contrary to international law.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls

events.title: World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas
events.description: UN partners launched the Winter Response Plan to help 2.1 million people in Ukraine survive another cold season as the country grapples with the on-going Russian invasion.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas

events.title: Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada
events.description: Fires remain out of control in Northwest Territories and Ontario, while Argentina endures record night-time heat Recent hot, dry weather in Canada has triggered hundreds of wildfires across the country. As of Wednesday, more than 900 were burning, scorching about 3m hectares of land. While fires have broken out in several provinces, the greatest concern is in the Northwest Territories and Ontario , where several remain out of control. Poor air quality has been reported across affected regions, with wildfire smoke carried by winds into the US and even across the Atlantic to northern Europe. The risk of further blazes remains high as North America enters the peak of fire season. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada

events.title: Wildfires in Europe prompt tens of thousands to evacuate
events.description: Spain has declared a national emergency in the Madrid region. In neighboring France, meanwhile, President Emmanuel Macron has invoked the EU's civil protection mechanism to ask for help.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires in Europe prompt tens of thousands to evacuate

events.title: Spain: Thousands evacuate as heatwave fuels blazes near Madrid
events.description: Thousands of people were forced to flee deadly wildfires that continued to sweep through parts of France, Spain and Italy on Friday. Hundreds of firefighters backed by water-bombing planes battled the blazes fanned by scorching temperatures and strong winds. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Spain: Thousands evacuate as heatwave fuels blazes near Madrid

events.title: Live: Thousands forced to flee as deadly wildfires rage in parts of France, Italy and Spain
events.description: Thousands of people were forced to flee deadly wildfires that continued to sweep through parts of France, Spain and Italy on Friday. Hundreds of firefighters backed by water-bombing planes battled the blazes fanned by scorching temperatures and strong winds. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Live: Thousands forced to flee as deadly wildfires rage in parts of France, Italy and Spain

events.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
events.description: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alongside his wife , Rosario Murillo, as “co-president”. Continue reading...

events.title: Major fire after Ukrainian strike on St Petersburg warehouse
events.description: A major fire broke out at a Wildberries e-commerce warehouse in St Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone attack.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Major fire after Ukrainian strike on St Petersburg warehouse

events.title: US threatens 'major military punishment' for Houthi attacks
events.description: President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iran and the Houthi militants over attacks on Saudi tankers. Meanwhile, the UN warned that the situation is "out of control." DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US threatens 'major military punishment' for Houthi attacks

events.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
events.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

events.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
events.description: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat

events.title: Middle East live: US launches new strikes on Iran as oil prices skyrocket
events.description: The United States launched a new series of strikes in Iran on Friday, while Iran-backed Houthi rebels opened a second front in the conflict, attacking Saudi tankers in the Red Sea and sending oil prices skyrocketing. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East live: US launches new strikes on Iran as oil prices skyrocket

events.title: Fate of ICC chief prosecutor heads to unprecedented vote as US sanctions keep him out of New York
events.description: Diplomats from around the world are gathering at United Nations headquarters in New York on Friday to decide whether to permanently remove the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court

events.title: Guterres calls for stepping up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran conflict
events.description: The United Nations again called for restraint as war ramps up in the Middle East, underscoring the need for dialogue and an end to attacks on “vital infrastructure” across the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres calls for stepping up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran conflict

events.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
events.description: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International Bridge A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods. In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies. Continue reading...

events.title: India: Wangchuk ends hunger strike, clears way for government-CJP talks
events.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike 'in view of possible violence in the country,' paving way for dialogue between Modi's government and Cockroach party leaders. Meanwhile, protests continue. Follow DW
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India: Wangchuk ends hunger strike, clears way for government-CJP talks

events.title: US hits partners with new tariffs over forcer labour claims
events.description: Starting this Friday, the United States will impose new tariffs on imports from 60 trading partners, covering 99 percent of all goods entering the country. The new measures replace the temporary 10 percent global tariff introduced 150 days ago after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's previous sweeping tariff plan. The administration says the latest tariffs are aimed at addressing concerns over goods produced with forced labour. Peter O'Brien reports.

events.title: Fields medal honours four mathematicians as AI reshapes the profession
events.description: The International Mathematical Union awarded the prestigious Fields Medal to four mathematicians on Thursday, including only the third woman to receive the honour, as researchers weighed the growing impact of artificial intelligence on a field increasingly being reshaped by the technology.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fields medal honours four mathematicians as AI reshapes the profession

events.title: Surveillance video shows officers tackle 'anti-American' suspect after Federal Building firebombing

events.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal: How will it impact Middle East, US-Iran war?

events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Saudi nuclear deal: How will it impact Middle East, US-Iran war?

events.title: Global hunger declines again but progress remains uneven across regions
events.description: Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025 showing that progress is possible, but gains remain fragile and far too slow to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by the 2030 deadline.

events.title: Multiple injured after floatplane makes emergency landing, catches fire off Washington coast

events.title: UK's Burnham names Trump critic Ed Miliband foreign secretary; ex-Thatcher aide brands pick 'disastrous'

events.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
events.description: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds

events.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results

events.title: At least 50 people killed and 700,000 displaced by India floods after monsoon rains
events.description: Officials in India say at least 50 people have died and 700,000 have been displaced by flooding after days of monsoon rains
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ At least 50 people killed and 700,000 displaced by India floods after monsoon rains

events.title: Julian Leeser says his son no longer feels safe at Jewish events – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian exports sent to the US will be hit with a 12.5% tariff as the US president imposes levies on more than 80 nations. The Trump administration unveiled the latest tranche of tariffs for countries over their “failure to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labour”. This, in our view, makes no sense. Forced labour is definitely something which needs to be combated around the world, so we support that objective. We absolutely need to see the strait of Hormuz be open and for freedom of navigation to happen there, for commercial shipping to be allowed through, and we very much call on Iran to allow that to be the case. We need to see a restoration to the normal global fuel supply chain because it’s having a significant impact around the world. Continue reading...

events.title: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike
events.description: Sonam Wangchuk has ended his hunger strike after 26 days, saying the decision follows a "long negotiation" on various conditions and "in view of possible violence in the country."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike

events.title: Iran urged to stop protest-related executions

events.title: Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action
events.description: In the Security Council debate on Thursday on how best to settle disputes between States peacefully, ambassadors provided examples of success and challenges ahead under such themes as effective early warning and how to move from rhetorical support for preventive diplomacy to ensuring more consistent use of its Chapter VI powers under the UN Charter.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action

events.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
events.description: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year. Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities. Continue reading...

events.title: Australian regulator TGA warns of ‘rare but severe’ eye disorder linked to GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic
events.description: TGA says product information for some diabetes and weight loss drugs updated with warning of NAION disorder Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s medicines regulator has issued warnings for several type 2 diabetes and obesity-related treatments – including Ozempic and Mounjaro – over the potential to cause a “rare but severe” eye disorder that could lead to blindness. The Therapeutic Goods Administration’s (TGA) warning came after several GLP-1 RA medicines marketed in Australia as Ozempic, Wegovy and Mounjaro, among other products, were linked to 36 cases of the condition. Continue reading...

events.title: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
events.description: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

events.title: New Caledonia: A deeply divided territory
events.description: Two years after the riots that shook New Caledonia in 2024, FRANCE 24 returns to a French overseas territory still grappling with the consequences of one of the darkest chapters in its recent history. Travelling from the Kanak independence stronghold of Saint-Louis to Mont-Dore, Nouméa and Bourail, our reporters meet the people living with the lasting scars of the violence.

events.title: Nicaragua's parliament plans to eliminate elections to codify President Ortega's declaration

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat

events.title: Mayor shot dead in Mexico months after surviving assassination attempt
events.description: Valentín Lavín Romero is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in 2024 A mayor was shot dead on Wednesday in the Mexican state of Morelos after having survived a previous assassination attempt earlier this year. He is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in October 2024. Valentín Lavín Romero, who was from the Green party, an ally of the ruling leftwing Morena party, was wounded at the end of January when hitmen shot him at a petrol station on a motorway. Continue reading...

events.title: Rubio says U.S. is ready to help end war in Ukraine but no quick deal ahead

events.title: Ghana needs laws to protect victims of witchcraft accusations

And The Kigali Cinema Junction is bringing five days of screenings, workshops and industry discussions to audiences across Kigali.

events.title: MoD cancels army training in Kenya over powers to prosecute British soldiers

events.title: Donald Trump hits Australian exports to US with new higher trade tariff over claims of ‘forced labour’

The United States announced on Friday morning (AEST) that many Australian exports now attract a new 12.5% tariff as part of a broader regime affecting dozens of countries. However the Albanese government says Australia’s three largest export categories – including beef and gold – will avoid the tariff hit. Continue reading...

events.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
events.description: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue reading...

events.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger in Paris: 'There's no single blueprint for Democratic Party'
events.description: A rising star among Democratic Party moderates, Virginia’s first woman governor Abigail Spanberger was elected the same night last November as Democratic Socialist New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani. In a wide-ranging interview with François Picard, Spanberger outlines a pragmatic vision of politics rooted in coalition building, institutional trust, and solving voters’ everyday concerns through practical governance rather than ideological confrontation. She argues that electoral success begins with engaging directly with people, going door to door and listening to their priorities.

events.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
events.description: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the west to Dunedin city in the east – will be plunged into daytime darkness for 2 minutes and 51 seconds. Continue reading...

events.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
events.description: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode sperm whale communication, spent four years tuning in on the calls of 15 sperm whales off the coast of Dominica. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover

events.title: How vanilla's history is not so … 'vanilla'
events.description: Long before "vanilla" meant bland, the bean's fandom spanned Mesoamerican elites to European courts. But it owes its ubiquity to a 12-year-old, enslaved boy.

events.title: Germany: Friedrich Merz announces Cabinet reshuffle
events.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has announced a Cabinet reshuffle in the wake of the resignation of conservative heavyweight Jens Spahn over a surrogacy controversy. More changes are expected in the coming days.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany: Friedrich Merz announces Cabinet reshuffle

events.title: Germany seeks energy and trading partners in Africa
events.description: Germany wants to boost relations with Africa. During his trip, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has promoted cooperation on energy, infrastructure and the economy. He also criticized US treatment of South Africa.

events.title: New PM cuts business rates for UK pubs in latest cost-of-living measure
events.description: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues in England on Thursday, his third major cost-of-living measure in three days as the new Labour leader seeks to revive the economy and support struggling high streets.

events.title: US imposes new tariffs on 60 countries - including UK
events.description: The Trump administration has imposed new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 countries over claims they failed to prevent forced labour.

events.title: US Virgin Islands faces second lawsuit over right to bear arms as Florida resident seeks license
events.description: Gun Owners of America and Gun Owners Foundation are suing the U.S. Virgin Islands and its police commissioner as a fight over the Second Amendment deepens

events.title: FBI Director Patel returns looted cultural artifacts to Indonesian President Prabowo
events.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto has received a collection of repatriated cultural artifacts from FBI Director Kash Patel during a meeting in Jakarta

events.title: Accused killer's alleged disguises unravel as sheriff releases new clues in month-long manhunt
events.description: Walton County Sheriff's Office reveals distinctive tattoos on fugitive Isabelle Johnson as the monthlong manhunt for the murder suspect continues.

events.title: ICE arrests Southwest Airlines flight attendant in Nashville for allegedly overstaying visa since 2021
events.description: Lorenzo Thompson, a Jamaican citizen and Southwest Airlines flight attendant, was arrested by ICE in Nashville after allegedly overstaying his visa.

events.title: Alex Murdaugh ranks among South Carolina’s worst tax deadbeats while awaiting new murder trial
events.description: South Carolina revenue records list Alex Murdaugh's $1.6 million tax debt as his lawyers push to test unknown male DNA found under Maggie's fingernails.

events.title: Florida woman on the run and wanted for murder relied on network of alleged accomplices to evade capture: Cops
events.description: Isabelle Rose Johnson allegedly uses disguises and aliases to evade arrest after Jason Christopher Coulthart's remains were found in Freeport, Florida.

events.title: $290M cocaine haul found ‘skillfully concealed’ in massive banana shipment
events.description: Italian officials said the 1,700-pound haul of cocaine, hidden among banana pallets, could have generated roughly $290 million on the street.

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.
